title: if/elsif chain: regex validation scans wrong Str
description: Trigger: compile("if header :regex \"Subject\" \"[invalid\" { keep; } elsif header :is \"T\" \"key\" { keep; }"). validate_stmt scans the entire combined if/elsif statement for the last Str/StringList, finds Str(\"key\") from the elsif clause instead of the actual regex Str(\"[invalid\"). The invalid regex is never validated at compile time. At runtime, the regex match falls back to on-the-fly compilation which fails, so the test always returns false (silent mismatch). Same root cause affects collect_regex_patterns: the regex pattern is never pre-cached, and the wrong string (the elsif key) is cached instead.
